acceptance_criteria.when: a reader inspects the dispatch payload body in Step 8
acceptance_criteria.then: the literal string 'baseline_head_sha' appears inside the dispatch payload block (not only in a comment), AND 'baseline_dirty_snapshot' also appears in the same block

check.expect: match in dispatch payload body, not comment-only

acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the dev.files_modified and dev.files_created field descriptions
acceptance_criteria.then: the description explicitly states these lists must be derived from 'git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha>' comparing working tree to baseline, AND the schema includes an 'observed_preexisting[]' field with description, AND 'baseline_head_sha' appears as a top-level dev-report JSON field

check.expect: all three patterns present in Output Format section

acceptance_criteria.given: agents/qa.md with a provenance check section
acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the provenance check procedure
acceptance_criteria.then: the text specifies: compute git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> (working tree vs baseline); for every path in dev.files_modified absent from this diff AND absent from baseline_dirty_snapshot, raise a critical finding with primary_cause: 'dev_implementation' and label 'dev_provenance_violation'

check.expect: all three patterns present in provenance check section

acceptance_criteria.when: a reader reads the dev-report enrichment procedure
acceptance_criteria.then: the text specifies that paths in dev.files_modified/dev.files_created absent from the baseline diff AND not in baseline_dirty_snapshot are classified as 'provenance_anomaly' and excluded from commit-message type/scope/summary derivation

check.expect: both patterns present in Phase 2 enrichment section

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report where baseline_head_sha is null or absent
acceptance_criteria.when: QA and changelog-analyst read their respective provenance check/classification rules
acceptance_criteria.then: the text in both agents/qa.md and agents/changelog-analyst.md specifies that the provenance check is skipped with a warning (not FAIL) when baseline_head_sha is absent

check.expect: graceful skip / warning language when baseline_head_sha is absent
